import pandas as pd
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def task_func(s1, s2, n_clusters=3):
    if not (isinstance(s1, pd.Series) and isinstance(s2, pd.Series)):
        raise ValueError("s1 and s2 must be pandas Series")

    if len(s1) != len(s2):
        raise ValueError("s1 and s2 must have the same length")

    combined_data = pd.concat([s1, s2], axis=1).values.flatten()
    kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=n_clusters)
    kmeans.fit(combined_data)

    cluster_labels = kmeans.labels_
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
    ax.scatter(s1, s2, c=cluster_labels)

    # Adding plot title and legend
    ax.set_title("K-Means Clustering")
    ax.legend(kmeans.cluster_centers_.T.astype(int))

    return tuple((cluster_labels, ax))
